name: Greater Pittsburgh Quick Cash
description_short: Greater Pittsburgh Quick Cash is a pawn shop and FFL firearms dealer in Duquesne, PA that buys, sells, and trades firearms, tools, jewelry, electronics, and collectibles.
description_long:
Greater Pittsburgh Quick Cash operates as a traditional pawn shop with an emphasis on firearms sales and transfers. Located at 1000 Duquesne Boulevard in Duquesne, Pennsylvania—walking distance from Kennywood Park—the business functions as both a retail pawn operation and a licensed FFL (Federal Firearms License) dealer. The company has established an online presence through their website and social media channels to advertise inventory and services to the Pittsburgh-area community.

The company offers a broad range of pawn and buy-sell-trade services across multiple categories. Their primary focus includes firearms (handguns and long guns), ammunition, and firearm accessories, along with FFL transfer services. Beyond firearms, they actively buy and sell tools (both new and used, hand and power tools), precious metals (gold, silver, diamonds), jewelry, coins, collectibles, electronics (TVs, laptops, newer models), music equipment, housewares, movies, knives, and luxury items. They also accept purses, wallets, and perfume for trade.

Greater Pittsburgh Quick Cash distinguishes itself through its specialized FFL dealer licensing, allowing them to handle firearm transfers and provide firearms expertise—a service not available at standard pawn shops. Their convenient location near a major regional attraction and their stated emphasis on acquiring high-end luxury items suggest they target both casual sellers and collectors. The business maintains extended hours (10 AM–6 PM, Monday through Saturday) to serve walk-in traffic.

As a pawn shop, the company provides liquidity through collateral-based lending, though their website does not explicitly detail loan terms, interest rates, or repayment structures. Customers should be aware that pawn transactions typically involve significant markups on resale and immediate cash trades that may undervalue items. The website lacks transparency on loan-to-value ratios or APR disclosures, which is standard for the pawn industry but important for consumers to understand before engaging.
pros: Licensed FFL dealer offering firearms transfers and professional firearm sales in addition to standard pawn services
Broad inventory across 12+ categories including tools, jewelry, electronics, music equipment, and collectibles—not limited to just firearms
Convenient location walking distance from Kennywood Park with extended hours (10 AM–6 PM, six days per week)
Online inventory showcase and multiple social media presence (Facebook, Armslist) for browsing before visiting
Buys high-end luxury items, precious metals, and collectibles—not just typical pawn shop inventory
Local, established business in the Pittsburgh area serving the community
Accepts a diverse range of items for purchase, trade, or pawn including used and new tools
cons: No disclosed loan terms, interest rates, APR, or loan-to-value ratios on the website—standard pawn markups apply but not transparent
Limited online shopping capability; website serves primarily as an inventory showcase rather than functional e-commerce platform
Closed Sundays, limiting accessibility for some consumers despite six-day weekly schedule
No information on authentication or grading standards for jewelry, collectibles, or precious metals—important for fair valuation
Firearms focus may deter customers seeking non-gun collateral loans or trades
best_for: Firearm owners and enthusiasts needing FFL transfers or looking to buy, sell, or trade guns and ammunition
Tool professionals and contractors seeking to sell or trade power tools and hand tools for quick cash
Local Pittsburgh-area residents with jewelry, precious metals, or collectibles needing immediate cash
Buyers searching for discounted used electronics, musical equipment, or luxury goods
diagnosis: Greater Pittsburgh Quick Cash is best suited for Pittsburgh-area residents and firearm enthusiasts seeking quick cash through pawn or collateral-based lending, particularly those with firearms, tools, jewelry, or electronics. The primary caveat is that pawn shops typically involve significant markups and item devaluation; customers should expect to receive 40–60% of an item's retail value and should comparison-shop before pawning or selling high-value items.
services: Firearm purchases and sales (handguns and long guns)
Pawn loans on firearms and other collateral
Tool buying and selling (new and used, hand and power tools)
General buy-sell-trade across housewares, knives, purses, wallets, and perfume
